template_name: GovBid Innovations LLC — Coverage Bundle (Project Attachment)
purpose: Authoritative geography eligibility controls for BID/NO-BID gating.
Prevents misuse of non-authoritative 'pins', 'authorities', and map labels as coverage evidence.
read_order: 2) Use this Coverage Bundle only for geographic eligibility gating and coverage evidence rules.
3) If any conflict exists, Prime Directive controls.

bid_gating_rules.hard_rule: Only states_by_name and counties_by_fips may be used to gate BID/NO-BID. Pins and map labels are visualization/reference only and must never be treated as coverage proof.
output_requirement: When declaring an opportunity 'in coverage' or 'out of coverage', cite the authoritative list item used (state name or county FIPS).
If coverage cannot be determined from authoritative lists, output: NEEDS-DATA and specify what is missing.

pins_authorities.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (NY)
pins_authorities.place_query: Metropolitan Transportation Authority, New York, NY

pins_authorities.label: Massachusetts Water Resources Authority
pins_authorities.place_query: Massachusetts Water Resources Authority, Boston, MA

pins_authorities.label: Memphis–Shelby County Airport Authority
pins_authorities.place_query: Memphis-Shelby County Airport Authority, Memphis, TN

pins_authorities.label: Schenectady City School District (NY)
pins_authorities.place_query: Schenectady City School District, Schenectady, NY

map_labels.description: City/place labels are informational only and must not be used as coverage or eligibility data.

anomaly_log.issue: Authority pin references Tennessee (Memphis) but Tennessee is not in coverage.states_by_name allowlist.
anomaly_log.impact: Could be misinterpreted as covered geography if pins are incorrectly used for gating.
mitigation: If Tennessee is truly covered, update the LOCKED profile and then update states_by_name accordingly via an explicit 'Update locked profile' process.

change_log.summary: Recreated as a Project attachment: simplified to gating controls + authoritative lists; kept anomaly guardrails; clarified output requirements.
